file_name: HEMISPHERX - Sales, Marketing, Distribution, and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: C. SCIEN shall pay HEMISPHERX for each order of Product within 75 days after receipt of the goods except for the for first purchase order which will be for 50 vials of Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived) ("First Order") and paid once the MOH approves the use for Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived) on 5 MERS patients. All purchase orders are final.
perturbation.changed_text: C. SCIEN shall pay HEMISPHERX for each order of Product within 180 days after receipt of the goods except for the for first purchase order which will be for 50 vials of Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived) ("First Order") and paid once the MOH approves the use for Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived) on 5 MERS patients. All purchase orders are final.
perturbation.explanation: Extended payment terms beyond what is commercially reasonable can be seen as an unfair trade practice, potentially conflicting with regulations that promote fair competition and timely payment for goods.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission
perturbation.law_explanation: Unfair methods of competition are unlawful and that an excessive payment window can be seen as an abusive business practice. The original payment term of 75 days is reasonable. Extending to 180 days creates an unfair burden on the supplier (HEMISPHERX) by restricting their capital flow.

perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the payment terms from 75 days to 180 days, which could be viewed as an unfair method of competition. The original text is reasonable whereas the extended time places an unfair burden on the supplier. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 15 USC 45, declares "Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission". Extending payment terms significantly beyond what is commercially reasonable can hinder a supplier's capital flow and be deemed an abusive practice, thus contradicting the law by potentially creating an unfair competitive environment.

perturbation.original_text: B. Such Confidential Information will be kept confidential until 5 years after the expiration of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: B. Such Confidential Information will be kept confidential until 1 year after the expiration of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period may create issues with trade secret protection, since the statute of limitations for misappropriation of trade secrets can be longer than one year. Therefore the term would provide inadequate protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: The one year confidentiality period following termination may not be sufficient to protect trade secrets, as the statute of limitations for misappropriation can be longer, such as five years, undermining the legal protection intended by trade secret law.

(2) Civil seizure.- (A) In
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text reduces the confidentiality period from 5 years to 1 year, which can be problematic for trade secret protection as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation. The original text provided a longer confidentiality window, whereas the modified text significantly shortens it, potentially undermining the intended legal protection under trade secret law. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 18 USC 1836, discusses civil proceedings for violations related to trade secrets, which implicitly acknowledges the importance of protecting trade secrets through legal means. Although the snippet does not specify a particular timeframe, the one-year confidentiality period in the modified contract could be insufficient to address misappropriation, thus creating a legal contradiction as the period might expire before misappropriation is discovered or pursued legally.

perturbation.original_text: M. In the event of termination of this license for any reason, SCIEN shall within 6months (as described in the Termination clause), cease all use of the "Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived)". SCIEN shall not thereafter use any names, mark or trade name similar thereto belonging to HEMISPHERX. Termination of the license under the provisions of this Agreement shall be without prejudice to any rights which HEMISPHERX may otherwise have against SCIEN.
perturbation.changed_text: M. In the event of termination of this license for any reason, SCIEN shall within 1 month (as described in the Termination clause), cease all use of the "Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived)". SCIEN shall not thereafter use any names, mark or trade name similar thereto belonging to HEMISPHERX. Termination of the license under the provisions of this Agreement shall be without prejudice to any rights which HEMISPHERX may otherwise have against SCIEN.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the transition period for ceasing the use of licensed trademarks to 1 month may not provide sufficient time for SCIEN to exhaust existing inventory and re-brand, potentially leading to economic hardship and accusations of unfair business practices.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 1114 - Remedies; infringement; innocent infringement
perturbation.law_explanation: A 1 month cessation period may be deemed commercially unreasonable, increasing the risk of inadvertent trademark infringement due to the inability to effectively exhaust inventory and cease using the mark in all marketing materials. This contradicts the standard for orderly termination and risk mitigation in trademark law.
perturbation.location: XII. PROSECUTION, INFRINGEMENT, AND DEFENSE OF HEMISPHERX INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

Remedies; infringement; innocent infringement by printers and publishers (1) Any person who shall, without the consent of the registrant- (a) use in commerce any reproduction, counterfeit, copy, or colorable imitation of a registered mark in connection with the sale, offering for sale, distribution, or advertising of any g
perturbation.justification: The modification reduces the time for SCIEN to cease using licensed trademarks from 6 months to 1 month. The Law Explanation states that "A 1 month cessation period may be deemed commercially unreasonable, increasing the risk of inadvertent trademark infringement." This suggests that the reduced timeframe could lead to a violation of trademark law, specifically regarding infringement, as the company may not be able to exhaust inventory and cease using the mark in all marketing materials within the shorter period. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 15 USC 1114, addresses remedies for trademark infringement, indicating the legal ramifications of unauthorized use of a registered mark. The Perturbation Explanation also suggests potential economic hardship and unfair business practices. Therefore, the change creates a legal contradiction by increasing the risk of trademark infringement due to a commercially unreasonable cessation period.
